data.ecs-operations#ECS operations: TODO: Tabular overview of the runtime cost of typical ECS operations. See Arche’s benchmarks for an example.
data.versus-array-of-structs#Versus Array of:
The plots below show the iteration time per entity in the classical Position-Velocity example. That is, iterate all entities with components Position and Velocity, and add velocity to position:
position.x += velocity.x position.y += velocity.y The benchmark is performed with different amounts of “payload components”, where each of them has two Float64 fields, just like Position and Velocity. Further, the total number of entities is varied from 100 to 1 million.
Note that the benchmarks run in the Github CI, which uses very powerfull hardware. Particularly, the processors have 256MB of cache. On a laptop or desktop computer with typically much less cache, Larecs🌲 will outperform AoS for everything but the smallest setups.

data.aliases#Aliases: MutableEntityAccessor = EntityAccessor[True, ?, ?, component_manager=?]: An entity accessor with mutable references to the components. ComponentType = CollectionElement
data.larecs#\u003ccode\u003elarecs\u003c/code:
Mojo package 🡭
larecsLarecs🌲 is a performance-oriented archetype-based ECS for Mojo.
It is based on the ECS Arche, implemented in the Go programming language.
Larecs🌲 is still under construction, so the API might change in future versions. It can, however, already be used for testing purposes.
Example:
# Import the package from larecs import World # Define components @value struct Position: var x: Float64 var y: Float64 @value struct IsStatic: pass @value struct Velocity: var x: Float64 var y: Float64 # Run the ECS fn main() raises: # Create a world, list all components that will / may be used world = World[Position, Velocity, IsStatic]() for _ in range(100): # Add an entity. The returned value is the # entity's ID, which can be used to access the entity later entity = world.add_entity(Position(0, 0), IsStatic()) # For example, we may want to change the entity's position world.get[Position](entity).x = 2 # Or we may want to replace the IsStatic component # of the entity by a Velocity component world.replace[IsStatic]().by(Velocity(2, 2), entity=entity) # We can query entities with specific components for entity in world.query[Position, Velocity](): # use get_ptr to get a pointer to a specific component position = entity.get_ptr[Position]() # use get to get a reference / copy of a specific component velocity = entity.get[Velocity]() position[].x += velocity.x position[].y += velocity.y
data.structs#Structs: EntityAccessor: Accessor for an Entity. Entity: Entity identifier. Holds an entity ID and it’s generation for recycling. Query: Query builder for entities with and without specific components. QueryInfo: Class that holds the same information as a query but no reference to the world. Resources: Manages resources. Scheduler: Manages the execution of systems in a world. TypeId: An ID to distinguish different types. Replacer: Replacer is a helper struct for removing and adding components to an Entity. World: World is the central type holding entity and component data, as well as resources.
data.traits#Traits: System: Trait for systems in the scheduler. IdentifiableCollectionElement: A Type that is uniquely identifiable via a given ID.

data.entity#\u003ccode\u003eEntity\u003c/code:
Mojo struct 🡭
Entity @register_passable_trivial struct Entity Entity identifier. Holds an entity ID and it’s generation for recycling.
Entities are only created via the World, using World.add_entity.
⚠️ Important: Entities are intended to be stored and passed around via copy, not via pointers! The zero value should be used to indicate “nil”, and can be checked with Entity.is_zero.

data.identifiablecollectionelement#\u003ccode:
Mojo trait 🡭
IdentifiableCollectionElementA Type that is uniquely identifiable via a given ID.
By convention, the ID should contain the package, module, and class name. For example, the ID for a MyStruct in the module my_module in the package my_package would be assigned as follows:
struct MyStruct(IdentifiableCollectionElement): alias id = TypeId("my_package.my_module.MyStruct") If the ID is used in a limited context only, you can set the id manually by passing an integer to the constructor.

without fn without[*Ts: CollectionElement](owned self, out query: Query[world_origin, ComponentTypes, has_without_mask=True]) Excludes the given components from the query. world = World[Float64, Float32, Int]() _ = world.add_entity(Float64(1.0), Float32(2.0), 3) _ = world.add_entity(Float64(1.0), 3) for entity in world.query[Float64, Int]().without[Float32](): f = entity.get_ptr[Float64]() f[] += 1 Parameters:
*Ts (CollectionElement): The types of the components to exclude. Args:
self (Self) query (Query[world_origin, ComponentTypes, has_without_mask=True]) Returns:
Query[world_origin, ComponentTypes, has_without_mask=True]: The query, exclusing the given components.
exclusive fn exclusive(owned self, out query: Query[world_origin, ComponentTypes, has_without_mask=True]) Makes the query only match entities with exactly the query’s components. world = World[Float64, Float32, Int]() _ = world.add_entity(Float64(1.0), Float32(2.0), 3) _ = world.add_entity(Float64(1.0), 3) for entity in world.query[Float64, Int]().exclusive(): f = entity.get_ptr[Float64]() f[] += 1 Args:
self (Self) query (Query[world_origin, ComponentTypes, has_without_mask=True]) Returns:
Query[world_origin, ComponentTypes, has_without_mask=True]: The query, made exclusive.

data.query#\u003ccode\u003eQuery\u003c/code\u:
Mojo struct 🡭
Query @memory_only struct Query[world_origin: MutableOrigin, *ComponentTypes: CollectionElement, *, has_without_mask: Bool = False] Query builder for entities with and without specific components.
This type should not be used directly, but through the World.query method:
world = World[Float64, Float32, Int]() _ = world.add_entity(Float64(1.0), Float32(2.0), 3) _ = world.add_entity(Float64(1.0), 3) for entity in world.query[Float64, Int](): f = entity.get_ptr[Float64]() f[] += 1

data.queryinfo#\u003ccode\u003eQueryInfo\u003:
Mojo struct 🡭
QueryInfo @memory_only struct QueryInfo[has_without_mask: Bool = False] Class that holds the same information as a query but no reference to the world.
This struct can be constructed implicitly from a Query instance. Therefore, Query instances can be used instead of QueryInfo in function arguments.

self (Self) entity (Entity): The entity to modify. *components (*AddTs): The components to add. Raises:

Error: when called with components that can’t be removed because they are not present. Error: when called on a locked world. Do not use during World.query iteration.

self (Self) steps (Int): How often the systems should be updated. Raises:

self (Self) Raises:
run fn run(mut self, steps: Int) Runs the scheduler for a given number of steps. This is the main entry point for running the scheduler. It calls the initialize, update, and finalize methods in order. The update method is called steps times. The initialize method is called once at the beginning, and the finalize method is called once at the end.
Args:
self (Self) steps (Int): The number of steps to run. Raises:
data.scheduler#\u003ccode\u003eScheduler\u003:
Mojo struct 🡭
Scheduler @memory_only struct Scheduler[*ComponentTypes: CollectionElement] Manages the execution of systems in a world.
The systems must implement System. Usage example:
Example:
@value struct MySystem: var internal_variable: Int # This is executed once at the beginning fn initialize(mut self, mut world: World) raises: _ = world.add_entities(Position(0.0, 0.0), Velocity(1.0, 1.0), count=10) # This is executed in each step fn update(mut self, mut world: World) raises: for entity in world.query[Position, Velocity](): entity.get[Position]().x += entity.get[Velocity]().x entity.get[Position]().y += entity.get[Velocity]().y # This is executed at the end fn finalize(mut self, mut world: World) raises: print("Final positions") for entity in world.query[Position](): print(entity.get[Position]().x, entity.get[Position]().y) scheduler = Scheduler[Position, Velocity]() scheduler.add_system(MySystem(internal_variable=42)) scheduler.run(10)

data.typeid#\u003ccode\u003eTypeId\u003c/code:
Mojo struct 🡭
TypeId @register_passable_trivial struct TypeId An ID to distinguish different types.
By convention, every type implementing .IdentifiableCollectionElement should have a TypeId that contains its package, module, and class name. For example, the ID for a MyStruct in the module my_module in the package my_package would be assigned as follows:
struct MyStruct(IdentifiableCollectionElement): alias id = TypeId("my_package.my_module.MyStruct") If the ID is used in a limited context only, you can set the id manually by passing an integer to the constructor.

add_entity fn add_entity[*Ts: CollectionElement](mut self, *components: *Ts, out entity: Entity) Returns a new or recycled Entity. The given component types are added to the entity. Do not use during World.query iteration!
⚠️ Important: Entities are intended to be stored and passed around via copy, not via pointers! See Entity.
Example:
world = World[Position, Velocity]() e = world.add_entity( Position(0, 0), Velocity(0.5, -0.5), ) Parameters:
*Ts (CollectionElement): The components to add to the entity. Args:
self (Self) *components (*Ts): The components to add to the entity. entity (Entity) Returns:
Entity: The new or recycled Entity.
Raises:
Error: If the world is locked.
add_entities fn add_entities[*Ts: CollectionElement](mut self, *components: *Ts, *, count: UInt, out iterator: _ArchetypeEntityIterator[self._archetypes, self._locks, component_types, component_manager=ComponentManager()]) Adds a batch of Entitys. The given component types are added to the entities. Do not use during World.query iteration!
Example:
world = World[Position, Velocity]() for entity in world.add_entities( Position(0, 0), Velocity(0.5, -0.5), count = 5 ): # Do things with the newly created entities position = entity.get[Position]() Parameters:
*Ts (CollectionElement): The components to add to the entity. Args:
self (Self) *components (*Ts): The components to add to the entity. count (UInt): The number of entities to add. iterator (_ArchetypeEntityIterator[self._archetypes, self._locks, component_types, component_manager=ComponentManager()]) Returns:
_ArchetypeEntityIterator[self._archetypes, self._locks, component_types, component_manager=ComponentManager()]: An iterator to the new or recycled Entitys.
Raises:
Error: If the world is locked.
remove_entity fn remove_entity(mut self, entity: Entity) Removes an Entity, making it eligible for recycling. Do not use during World.query iteration!
Args:
self (Self) entity (Entity): The entity to remove. Raises:
Error: If the world is locked or the entity does not exist.
remove_entities fn remove_entities(mut self, query: QueryInfo[has_without_mask]) Removes multiple entities based on the provided query, making them eligible for recycling. Example:
world = World[Float32, Float64]() _ = world.add_entity(Float32(0)) _ = world.add_entity(Float32(0), Float64(0)) _ = world.add_entity(Float64(0)) # Remove all entities with a Float32 component. world.remove_entities(world.query[Float32]()) Args:
self (Self) query (QueryInfo[has_without_mask]): The query to determine which entities to remove. Note, you can either use Query or QueryInfo. Raises:
Error: If the world is locked.
is_alive fn is_alive(self, entity: Entity) -> Bool Reports whether an Entity is still alive. Args:
self (Self) entity (Entity): The entity to check. Returns:
Bool
has fn has[T: CollectionElement](self, entity: Entity) -> Bool Returns whether an Entity has a given component. Parameters:
T (CollectionElement): The type of the component. Args:
self (Self) entity (Entity): The entity to check. Returns:
Bool
Raises:
Error: If the entity does not exist.
get fn get[T: CollectionElement](mut self, entity: Entity) -> ref [*[0,0]._archetypes._data] T Returns a reference to the given component of an Entity. Parameters:
T (CollectionElement): The type of the component. Args:
self (Self) entity (Entity) Returns:
ref [*[0,0]._archetypes._data] T
Raises:
Error: If the entity is not alive or does not have the component.
get_ptr fn get_ptr[T: CollectionElement](mut self, entity: Entity) -> Pointer[T, self._archetypes._data] Returns a pointer to the given component of the Entity. Parameters:
T (CollectionElement): The type of the component. Args:
self (Self) entity (Entity): The entity to get the component from. Returns:
Pointer[T, self._archetypes._data]
Raises:
Error: If the entity is not alive or does not have the component.
set fn set[T: CollectionElement](mut self, entity: Entity, owned component: T) Overwrites a component for an Entity, using the given content. Parameters:
T (CollectionElement): The type of the component. Args:
self (Self) entity (Entity): The entity to modify. component (T): The new component. Raises:
Error: If the Entity does not exist.
fn set[*Ts: CollectionElement](mut self, entity: Entity, owned *components: *Ts) Overwrites a component for an Entity, using the given content. Parameters:
*Ts (CollectionElement): The types of the components. Args:
self (Self) entity (Entity): The entity to modify. *components (*Ts): The new components. Raises:
Error: If the entity does not exist or does not have the component.
add fn add[*Ts: CollectionElement](mut self, entity: Entity, *add_components: *Ts) Adds components to an Entity. Parameters:
*Ts (CollectionElement): The types of the components to add. Args:
self (Self) entity (Entity): The entity to modify. *add_components (*Ts): The components to add. Raises:
Error: when called for a removed (and potentially recycled) entity. Error: when called with components that can’t be added because they are already present. Error: when called on a locked world. Do not use during World.query iteration.
fn add[*Ts: CollectionElement](mut self, *add_components: *Ts, *, entity: Entity) Adds components to an Entity. Parameters:
*Ts (CollectionElement): The types of the components to add. Args:
self (Self) *add_components (*Ts): The components to add. entity (Entity): The entity to modify. Raises:
Error: when called for a removed (and potentially recycled) entity. Error: when called with components that can’t be added because they are already present. Error: when called on a locked world. Do not use during World.query iteration.
remove fn remove[*Ts: CollectionElement](mut self, entity: Entity) Removes components from an Entity. Parameters:
*Ts (CollectionElement): The types of the components to remove. Args:
self (Self) entity (Entity): The entity to modify. Raises:
Error: when called for a removed (and potentially recycled) entity. Error: when called with components that can’t be removed because they are not present. Error: when called on a locked world. Do not use during World.query iteration.
replace fn replace[*Ts: CollectionElement](mut self) -> Replacer[self, __len__(), component_types] Returns a Replacer for removing and adding components to an Entity in one go. Use as world.replace[Comp1, Comp2]().by(comp3, comp4, comp5, entity=entity).
The number of removed components does not need to match the number of added components.
Parameters:
*Ts (CollectionElement): The types of the components to remove. Args:
self (Self) Returns:
Replacer[self, __len__(), component_types]
apply fn apply[operation: fn[MutableOrigin, Variadic[CollectionElement], ComponentManager[$1]](accessor: EntityAccessor[True, $0, $1, component_manager=$2]) capturing -> None, *Ts: CollectionElement, *, unroll_factor: Int = 1](mut self) Applies an operation to all entities with the given components. Parameters:
operation (fn[MutableOrigin, Variadic[CollectionElement], ComponentManager[$1]](accessor: EntityAccessor[True, $0, $1, component_manager=$2]) capturing -> None): The operation to apply. *Ts (CollectionElement): The types of the components. unroll_factor (Int): The unroll factor for the operation (see vectorize doc). Args:
self (Self) Raises:
Error: If the world is locked.
fn apply[operation: fn[Int, MutableOrigin, Variadic[CollectionElement], ComponentManager[$2]](accessor: EntityAccessor[True, $1, $2, component_manager=$3]) capturing -> None, *Ts: CollectionElement, *, simd_width: Int = 1, unroll_factor: Int = 1](mut self) Applies an operation to all entities with the given components. The operation is applied to chunks of simd_width entities, unless not enough are available anymore. Then the chunk size simd_width is reduced.
Uses vectorize internally. Have a look there to see a more detailed explanation of the parameters simd_width and unroll_factor.
Caution! If simd_width is greater than 1, the operation must apply to the simd_width elements after the element passed to operation, assuming that each component is stored in contiguous memory. This may require knowledge of the memory layout of the components!
Example:
from sys.info import simdwidthof from memory import UnsafePointer world = World[Float64]() e = world.add_entity() fn operation[simd_width: Int](accessor: MutableEntityAccessor) capturing: # Define the operation to apply here. # Note that due to the immature # capturing system of Mojo, the world may be # accessible by copy capturing here, even # though it is not copyable. # Do NOT change `world` from inside the operation, # as it will not be reflected in the world # or may cause a segmentation fault. # Get the component try: component = accessor.get_ptr[Float64]() except: return # Get an unsafe pointer to the memory # location of the component ptr = UnsafePointer.address_of(component[]) # Load a SIMD of size `simd_width` # Note that a strided load is needed if the component as more than one field. val = ptr.load[width=simd_width]() # Do an operation on the SIMD val += 1 # Store the SIMD at the same address ptr.store(val) world.apply[operation, Float64, simd_width=simdwidthof[Float64]()]() Parameters:
operation (fn[Int, MutableOrigin, Variadic[CollectionElement], ComponentManager[$2]](accessor: EntityAccessor[True, $1, $2, component_manager=$3]) capturing -> None): The operation to apply. *Ts (CollectionElement): The types of the components. simd_width (Int): The SIMD width for the operation (see vectorize doc). unroll_factor (Int): The unroll factor for the operation (see vectorize doc). Args:
self (Self) Raises:
Error: If the world is locked.
query fn query[*Ts: CollectionElement](mut self, out iterator: Query[self, component_types]) Returns an Query for all Entities with the given components. Parameters:
*Ts (CollectionElement): The types of the components. Args:
self (Self) iterator (Query[self, component_types]) Returns:
Query[self, component_types]: A Query for all entities with the given components.
is_locked fn is_locked(self) -> Bool Returns whether the world is locked by any queries. Args:
self (Self) Returns:
Bool
data.world#\u003ccode\u003eWorld\u003c/code\u:
Mojo struct 🡭
World @memory_only struct World[*component_types: CollectionElement] World is the central type holding entity and component data, as well as resources.
The World provides all the basic ECS functionality of Larecs, like World.query, World.add_entity, World.add, World.remove, World.get or World.remove_entity.
